title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Classic Chicken Cacciatore Recipe (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
seo.metaDescription: Master classic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with our easy recipe. Tender chicken, rich tomato sauce & fresh herbs. Perfect for a hearty meal!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Pollo alla Cacciatora with bell peppers
h2Headings: What is Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken?

seo.imageAlt: A rustic plate of classic Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with tender chicken, bell peppers, and tomato sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Serve this hearty Italian Chicken Cacciatore with crusty bread or pasta for a comforting family dinner.

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, is a rustic Italian classic featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory tomato sauce with bell peppers, onions, and aromatic herbs. This hearty and comforting dish embodies the flavors of the Italian countryside, perfect for a satisfying family meal. Its robust flavors develop beautifully as it simmers, creating a truly authentic taste of Italy.

instructions.title: Prepare and Season Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and black pepper on all sides, ensuring even coverage for maximum flavor.

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken pieces, skin-side down, and sear until deeply golden brown and crispy, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove the browned chicken and set aside, leaving the rendered fat and flavorful bits in the pot.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Vegetables
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic, red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and sliced mushrooms. Sauté for another 5-7 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften and release their aromas.

instructions.title: Build the Sauce Base
instructions.text: If using, pour in the dry red wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot (deglazing) with a wooden spoon. Let it simmer for 2 minutes until reduced slightly. Stir in the tomato paste, canned diced tomatoes, chicken broth, fresh rosemary sprig, fresh thyme, and dried oregano. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer Pollo alla Cacciatora
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the simmering sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged to cook evenly. Reduce heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and easily pulls away from the bone.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the pitted Kalamata olives (if using) during the last 5 minutes of cooking to warm them through. Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed. Remove the rosemary sprig before serving. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley for a vibrant finish.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, marinate the chicken in red wine and a pinch of herbs for at least an hour, or even overnight, before browning.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, remove the chicken after simmering and reduce the sauce over high heat for a few minutes before returning the chicken to coat.

Pollo alla Cacciatora often tastes even better the next day, as the flavors meld and deepen. It's an excellent make-ahead meal; store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article:
Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, is a beloved classic in Italian cuisine, renowned for its rich, rustic flavors and comforting appeal. This hearty dish embodies the essence of traditional Italian cooking: simple, high-quality ingredients transformed into a meal that warms the soul. Perfect for a family dinner or a cozy gathering, Pollo alla Cacciatora offers a delicious escape to the Italian countryside with every bite. If you’re searching for an authentic Italian chicken recipe that’s both satisfying and relatively straightforward to prepare, look no further than this timeless favorite.

## What is Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken?

Pollo alla Cacciatora translates literally to "hunter's style chicken," a name that hints at its origins as a meal prepared with ingredients readily available to a hunter after a day in the field. This rustic dish typically features chicken braised in a savory sauce made from tomatoes, onions, bell peppers, garlic, and herbs. While regional variations exist across Italy, the core components remain consistent: tender chicken enveloped in a flavorful, aromatic sauce. It’s a dish that speaks of home-cooked comfort, designed to be substantial and deeply satisfying. Unlike some more delicate Italian preparations, Chicken Cacciatore is robust and hearty, making it an ideal choice for cooler weather or when you crave a truly fulfilling meal. Its beauty lies in its simplicity and the way the flavors meld and deepen as it slowly simmers, creating a truly memorable culinary experience.

## Ingredients You Need for Chicken Cacciatore

Crafting an authentic Pollo alla Cacciatora starts with selecting the right ingredients. For the chicken, bone-in, skin-on thighs and drumsticks are highly recommended as they impart more flavor and stay moist during the long cooking process. You'll need good quality olive oil for browning the chicken and sautéing the vegetables. Aromatic vegetables are key: a large onion, several cloves of garlic, and a mix of red and green bell peppers will form the flavorful base.

For the sauce, canned crushed or diced tomatoes are essential; choose a high-quality brand for the best results. A tablespoon or two of tomato paste will deepen the tomato flavor. Red wine, such as a dry Chianti or Merlot, is often used to deglaze the pan and adds wonderful complexity, though chicken broth can be used as a substitute for an alcohol-free version. Fresh herbs like rosemary, thyme, and oregano are crucial for that authentic Italian aroma and taste. Don't forget salt and freshly ground black pepper to season generously throughout the cooking process. A successful Chicken Cacciatore relies on the freshness and quality of these core components.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Pollo alla Cacciatora

Making Pollo alla Cacciatora is a rewarding process that fills your kitchen with incredible aromas. Here’s a detailed guide:

1.  **Prepare and Brown the Chicken:** Pat your chicken pieces dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper. Heat 2-3 tablespoons of olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken pieces, skin-side down first, until deeply golden brown on all sides. This step is crucial for developing rich flavor. Remove the browned chicken and set aside.
2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** Reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion and bell peppers to the pot, adding a little more olive oil if needed. Sauté until softened, about 8-10 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, allowing it to slightly caramelize.
3.  **Deglaze and Build the Sauce:** Pour in about ½ cup of red wine (if using) and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon. Let the wine reduce by half. Add the crushed or diced tomatoes, chicken broth, and your fresh herbs (a few sprigs of rosemary and thyme, and a teaspoon of dried oregano if not using fresh). Stir well, season with a pinch more salt and pepper, and bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
4.  **Braise the Chicken:** Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged. Bring the sauce back to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it braise for 45-60 minutes, or until the chicken is fork-tender and cooked through.
5.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the herb sprigs. Taste the sauce and adjust seasonings if necessary. Let the Pollo alla Cacciatora rest for a few minutes before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Cacciatore

Achieving an exceptional Chicken Cacciatore involves a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Don't Rush the Browning:** The initial browning of the chicken is paramount. A deep, golden-brown crust adds immense depth of flavor to the finished dish. Take your time to get a good sear on all sides.
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Since this dish relies on simple flavors, the quality of your ingredients makes a big difference. Invest in good canned tomatoes, fresh vegetables, and flavorful chicken.
*   **Fresh Herbs are Superior:** While dried herbs can be used, fresh rosemary and thyme truly elevate the aroma and taste of the sauce. Add them in sprigs and remove before serving.
*   **Slow Simmer for Flavor:** Allow the dish to simmer gently for at least 45 minutes to an hour. This slow cooking process allows the flavors to meld and the chicken to become incredibly tender.
*   **Make Ahead:** Like many braised dishes, Chicken Cacciatore often tastes even better the next day. The flavors continue to develop and deepen overnight, making it a fantastic meal prep option.

## Serving Suggestions for Hunter Style Chicken

Pollo alla Cacciatora is a complete meal in itself, but it pairs beautifully with a variety of side dishes that soak up its rich, savory sauce.

*   **Creamy Polenta:** The classic Italian accompaniment, creamy polenta is perfect for absorbing every drop of the delicious sauce.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A warm, crusty loaf of Italian bread is essential for mopping up the flavorful juices.
*   **Pasta:** Serve it over a bed of wide pasta noodles like tagliatelle or pappardelle, allowing the sauce to cling to every strand.
*   **Roasted Potatoes:** Simple roasted potatoes or even mashed potatoes provide a comforting side that complements the rustic nature of the dish.
*   **Simple Green Salad:** A light, crisp green salad with a vinaigrette dressing offers a refreshing contrast to the richness of the Chicken Cacciatore.

## Variations and Substitutions for Pollo alla Cacciatora

While the traditional recipe for Pollo alla Cacciatora is wonderful, it's also quite adaptable. Don't hesitate to experiment with these variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand.

*   **Chicken Cuts:** While bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces are ideal, you can use boneless, skinless chicken thighs for a quicker cooking time, or even chicken breasts, though they may dry out if overcooked.
*   **Additions:** Mushrooms, especially cremini or button mushrooms, are a popular addition, bringing an earthy depth. Olives (kalamata or green) and capers can also be stirred in during the last 15-20 minutes of cooking for a briny kick.
*   **Vegetables:** Feel free to add other vegetables like carrots, celery, or zucchini for extra flavor and nutrition.
*   **Wine Alternatives:** If you prefer not to use wine, simply increase the amount of chicken broth. A splash of balsamic vinegar can also add a touch of acidity and depth.
*   **Spice Level:** For those who enjoy a little heat, a pinch of red pepper flakes can be added with the garlic.
*   **Cooking Methods:** While traditionally made on the stovetop and oven, Chicken Cacciatore can also be adapted for a slow cooker or Instant Pot for hands-off convenience.

Pollo alla Cacciatora is more than just a recipe; it's a celebration of Italian comfort food. Its hearty flavors and tender chicken make it a dish that will undoubtedly become a favorite in your kitchen, bringing a taste of Italy right to your dining table.
